description:
Combined task covering both interactive affordances and layout for the mobile hamburger menu trigger.

INTERACTIVE AFFORDANCES (original FRAC-60 scope):
1. Add cursor-pointer to the hamburger trigger so the cursor changes on hover, matching other interactive elements.
2. Add a visible hover state (color shift / opacity / scale — match the pattern used elsewhere on the site).
3. Add a focus-visible ring for keyboard users.
4. Add an active state.
5. Verify the menu items INSIDE the open hamburger panel also have proper cursor + hover treatment, not just the trigger button.

ALIGNMENT & SIZING (merged from FRAC-69):
6. The hamburger icon must be vertically centered on the SAME horizontal axis as the 'Fractal Collective' wordmark in the navbar — both share one centered baseline.
7. Increase the hamburger icon size — it currently feels too small relative to the wordmark. Pick a size that feels proportionate AND meets a >= 44px tap target for accessibility.

WHERE: likely src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx or a MobileMenu component — confirm at planning time.

MOBILE-FIRST NOTE: cursor styling is desktop-only behavior (touch devices ignore it), but the rule still applies for desktop users hitting the mobile-style menu at narrow widths. Tap target sizing matters specifically for mobile.

OUT OF SCOPE: redesigning the menu itself — just affordances, alignment, and size.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Hamburger trigger shows cursor:pointer on hover.
- Visible hover state matching the rest of the site's interactive elements.
- Focus-visible ring works for keyboard users.
- Open-panel menu items also have proper cursor + hover treatment.
- Hamburger and 'Fractal Collective' wordmark share the same vertical center at mobile widths.
- Hamburger icon is visibly larger than current and feels proportionate to the wordmark.
- Tap target >= 44px.
title: Hamburger menu: alignment, sizing, and interactive affordances (cursor + hover + focus)
